text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic splenectomy, beginning with the dissection of ligaments surrounding a large recurrent cyst in the spleen. As the peritoneal attachments are freed, the cyst is visibly emptied.
The surgeon continues to empty the contents of the chronic cyst within the spleen, ensuring complete evacuation of the cystic fluid.
This cyst was recurrent, though it was aspirated five times ultrasonographically. It filled up every time and gave a lot of discomfort to the patient.
The surgeon uses the ERBI bipolar radio frequency guided instrument to take the splenic artery, effectively freeing the spleen from its attachments to the tail of the pancreas.

The surgeon uses a monopolar hook to dissect and cut the splenic artery, which is visibly isolated from surrounding tissues. The same instrument is employed to free the spleen and the large cyst from diaphragmatic attachments, completing the dissection.

The surgeon performs a laparoscopic splenectomy, dissecting the remaining attachments of the spleen from the diaphragm. The spleen and a large perisplenic cyst are removed through a small incision. The pulsating splenic artery is visible during the procedure.
